ddt:Target.Name: XBA _ LR0_LR1 Cluster BCM V2.6
ddt:Target.Description: 6-feb-14 ------------------- 8-May-2014 (V1.2) With JCI(Sumit) Cluster diagnostic input data Builder was modifed (Satya) ------------------- 9-May-2014 (V1.3) Add/Del BCM resources errors = 0 : 16-05-2014 --------------------- 19-May-2014 Maintainability report checked with X52 Cluster. -------------------- (naveen) V1.6 03-JUN-2014 Maintainability report and manual send modified with X52 BCM -------------------(naveen) V1.7 ----- not uploaded in central 06-Jun-2014 Unnecessary screens deleted 3B15 added compared to V1.7 file 2121 request changed (from 11 bytes to 1 byte) 3129, 3132 request deleted 10C0 added and a new button added in screen 2 times 10C0 requrest copied and pasted from T4 BCM Modified Drive style screen - 17-jun-14 created EFAIL TT screen with values - 17-jun-14 in "Data" -->Drive_style_red_threshold, Drive_style_yellow_threshold - 'format' value "d" deleted - (19-06-14) "speed_set_in_cluster" data added into "Output control screen" (30-06-14)--> added - "Lectures" screen from LR2 cluster DDT deleted screen "BCM CS Antidem" deleted screen "DiagHardware" red_threshold data added to button in drive style screen frame 2108 added ------------------------naveen V1.8 --- 30 jun 14 renamed file from 1.7 to 1.8 (1.7 was updated through a long way to make a difference the file is renamed) ------------------naveen V1.9 --- 10 jul 14 deleted 3B15 Peremetric alarm Read_DIAG_MUX_ON_IO -> Battvoltage -> formula C-> changed from 100 to 1000 deleted below swohn 3 status messages in Pixals IO Control -> data -> display check control command (high series) ONLY Color 1 ON ONLY Color 2 ON ONLY Color 3 ON 14-07-14 - Naveen deleted - 3B17, 3B18, 3B19, 3B3D, 3B42, 3B43, 3B4F, 3B5F -- 210F, 2137, 2152, 2154, 2155 25-09-14 - Naveen 3004, splitted for Renault and Nissan 17-10-2014 -> DID$2129 - Maintainability report checked for DID import 21-10-2014 -> DID$220124 DataRead.Airbag_Present (This request is added on configuration file request (This is NA for LR0 and LR1 ClusterBCM)) 2E012400 -> AIRBAG write command (This is NA for LR0 and LR1 ClusterBCM)) Drive style TT present (Mnemonic recoded to delete orange hilight) --------------------------------------------------------V2.1 - archana 04-11-2014 DTC 9532, DTC 9519, DTC 9567, DTC 9408, DTC 9412 updated : the DTCs are added for hiding Unknown DTC in DDT player Drive Telltale updated in Screen. ------11-11-2014 - Naveen EFAIL_electronic_failure_telltale_present -> updated in generic mnemonic name for configuration file purpose (Error raised while generating Config file) ------Prabu 11-11-2014, 2180 part number list updated for XBA ------Naveen 30 april - 2015 ------ All SOC screens updated with reference to X52, DIDs required for SOC INT are added from X52 DDT builder. ------V2.3 - naveen Drive style telltale present write command updated from 2.3 28-08-2015 10:51 -> request 31AA added, request 218F added (these are for identifying E0 version and bypass IMMO) ------------------------------------------------- V2.4 Naveen 05-11-2015 AIRBAG present parameter added + Soft reset button in general configuration screen ------------------------------------------------- V2.5 Thiyaheswaran B 15 Feb 2016 Added the following requests, with respective data. For accomodating new regulations. 1 220109 DataRead.FrontSBRInhibition_CF 2 2E010900 DataWrite.FrontSBRInhibition_CF 3 220110 DataRead.OverSpeedAlert_Configuration 4 2E011000 DataWrite.OverSpeedAlert_Configuration 5 220111 DataRead.OverSpeedAlert_Thresholds 6 2E01110000 DataWrite.OverSpeedAlert_Thresholds 7 220120 DataRead.Abs_Present 8 2E012000 DataWrite.Abs_Present 9 22021C DataRead.External_seat_belt_reminder 10 2E021C00 DataWrite.External_seat_belt_reminder --------------- V2.6 Vignesh KK 22/10/2018 Screens modified for regulation changes ABS Config error bit added External SBR data changed ----------------

Data.Name: ACTIVATE_INTERIOR_LIGHTING_CF
Data.Comment: Set when the interior lighting shall be configured.

Data.Name: ANTI_HIJACK_CF
Data.Comment: Set when the anti hijack (or single door unlocking) shall be configured.

Data.Name: ApprovalNumber.LowerPart
Data.Comment: Certificate

Data.Name: AT_CF
Data.Comment: Set when the automatic gear box shall be configured.

Data.Name: AUTO_RELOCK_TIMEOUT_CF
Data.Comment: Set <> 0 when the auto relock shall be configured. (typical value is 2 min)

Data.Name: AUTO_WINDOW_CLOSING_CF
Data.Comment: Set when the auto windows closing shall be configured.

Data.Name: BRAKE_ECU_PRESENT_CF
Data.Comment: Set when brake ecu is configured.

Data.Name: L2_1
Data.Description:
If bargraph display, bar 1 will flash is fuel level calculated is ? L2_1.

If pointer display, pointer must be set at 0/8 position (lower limit in the red zone – Reserve fuel side) if fuel level calculated = L2_1.

Data.Name: L2_2
Data.Description:
If bargraph display, bar 2 will light on if fuel level calculated is > L2_2.

If pointer display, pointer must be set at 1/8 position if fuel level calculated = L2_2.

Data.Name: L2_3
Data.Description:
If bargraph display, bar 3 will light on if fuel level calculated is > L2_3.

If pointer display, pointer must be set at 2/8 position if fuel level calculated = L2_3.

Data.Name: L2_4
Data.Description:
If bargraph display, bar 4 will light on if fuel level calculated is > L2_4.

If pointer display, pointer must be set at 3/8 position if fuel level calculated = L2_4.

Data.Name: L2_5
Data.Description:
If bargraph display, bar 5 will light on if fuel level calculated is > L2_5.

If pointer display, pointer must be set at 4/8 position if fuel level calculated = L2_5.

Data.Name: L2_6
Data.Description:
If bargraph display, bar 6 will light on if fuel level calculated is > L2_6.

If pointer display, pointer must be set at 5/8 position if fuel level calculated = L2_6.

Data.Name: L2_7
Data.Description:
If bargraph display, bar 7 will light on if fuel level calculated is > L2_7.

If pointer display, pointer must be set at 6/8 position if fuel level calculated = L2_7.

Data.Name: L2_8
Data.Description:
If bargraph display, bar 8 will light on if fuel level calculated is > L2_8.

If pointer display, pointer must be set at 7/8 position if fuel level calculated = L2_8.

Data.Name: L2_9
Data.Description:
If bargraph display, bar 9 will light on if fuel level calculated is > L2_9.

If pointer display, pointer must be set at 8/8 position if fuel level calculated = L2_9.

Data.Name: L2_2_GAS
Data.Description:
If bargraph display, bar 2 will light on if fuel level calculated is > L2_2.

If pointer display, pointer must be set at 1/8 position if fuel level calculated = L2_2.

Data.Name: L2_6_GAS
Data.Description:
If bargraph display, bar 6 will light on if fuel level calculated is > L2_6.

If pointer display, pointer must be set at 5/8 position if fuel level calculated = L2_6.

Data.Description: This configuration is used for the oil pressure warning. If the value = 11111b then cluster uses the CAN parameter for this warning (no else oil pressure sensor is an input of the cluster).

Data.Comment: This configuration is used to force the backlight of the cluster to be ON in day mode. This paramete
Data.Description: This configuration is used to force the backlight of the cluster to be ON in day mode. This parameter has no effect on night mode backlight management.

Device.Name: Seat belt telltale command monitoring (external telltale)

Screen.Name: After Sales BCM and CIDs PAIRING

Label.Text: AFTER SALES BCM or Customer IDs pairing
Complete the "After Sales Code" (16 bytes) and press the button "ENTER AFTER SALES MODE"
If the AFTER SALES MODE for BCM is launched, then put Ignition ON with a CID belonging to the vehicle
Press the button "CID PAIRING" for each CID to pair
Once all CIDs are paired, select "Validate" and press the button "EXIT AFTER SALES MODE"
Press the button "BCM PAIRING" to pair the BCM
If the BCM is paired, the EXIT of the AFTER SALES MODE is automatic. Then launch again the AFTER SALES MODE to do the pairing of all customers IDs
If the AFTER SALES MODE for CID is launched, then put Ignition ON with a CID (belonging to the vehicle or blank)
Click below button before you press play button on TOP
3) Complete the "After Sales Code" (16 bytes) and press the button "ENTER ECM AFTER SALES MODE"
4) If the authorization to pair the ECM is OK, put Ignition ON. Once the ECM is paired, the exit of the ECM AFTER SALES MODE is automatic
1) Put Ignition ON and check that the ECM is blank
To Cancel the authorization to pair the ECM, press the button "EXIT ECM AFTER SALES PAIRING"
Click below button before you press play button on TOP
